generated_oml:
instance InternalAirTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 High-Precision 1-Wire Digital Thermometer monitoring internal air temperature with ±0.5°C accuracy, placed inside the styrofoam box for real-time thermal monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalAirTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance HeatbedTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 High-Precision 1-Wire Digital Thermometer monitoring heatbed temperature with ±0.5°C accuracy, placed inside the styrofoam box to capture thermal inertia dynamics."
    DTDFVocab:producedData HeatbedTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance ExternalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 High-Precision 1-Wire Digital Thermometer monitoring ambient/external temperature with ±0.5°C accuracy, placed outside the styrofoam box to track environmental conditions."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ExternalTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance InternalAirTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Continuous temperature readings from internal air sensor transmitted via 1-Wire bus protocol to Raspberry Pi at high temporal resolution (unspecified frequency, likely seconds/sub-second intervals)."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalAirTemperatureSensor
]

instance HeatbedTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Continuous temperature readings from heatbed sensor transmitted via 1-Wire bus protocol to Raspberry Pi at high temporal resolution (unspecified frequency, likely seconds/sub-second intervals)."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HeatbedTemperatureSensor
]

instance ExternalTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Continuous ambient temperature readings transmitted via 1-Wire bus protocol to Raspberry Pi at high temporal resolution (unspecified frequency, likely seconds/sub-second intervals)."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ExternalTemperatureSensor
]

instance TwoParameterThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Lumped-parameter thermal model with 2 free parameters used for coarse-grained temperature prediction and control. Calibrated via comparison with real-time sensor data to minimize simulation-reality discrepancies."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler
]

instance FourParameterThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Lumped-parameter thermal model with 4 free parameters providing fine-grained thermal inertia prediction. Demonstrated superior accuracy in capturing heatbed dynamics compared to 2-parameter model."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler
]

instance RawTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Unprocessed temperature readings from all three sensors (internal air, heatbed, external) collected at high frequency for real-time monitoring and calibration."
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalAirTemperatureDataTransmitted, HeatbedTemperatureDataTransmitted, ExternalTemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreprocessingEnabler
]

instance CalibratedModelParameters : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Adjusted thermal model parameters (e.g., thermal conductivity, inertia) derived from calibration algorithms comparing simulated and real-world sensor values."
    DTDFVocab:fromData RawTemperatureData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler
]

instance SimulationOutputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Predicted temperature trajectories and thermal gradients generated by executing calibrated thermal models under current or hypothetical operating conditions."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CalibratedModelParameters
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelExecutionEnabler
]

instance RaspberryPiController : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Embedded Raspberry Pi platform serving as cyber-physical interface for real-time data acquisition, actuation, and basic processing. Implements 1-Wire protocol for sensor communication and bang-bang control logic."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, ControlAdjustmentService
]

instance PythonScipyStack : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python (Scipy) implementation for solving differential equations representing thermal dynamics and executing calibration algorithms to update model parameters based on sensor data."
    DTDFVocab:enables ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler
]

instance RabbitMQMessageBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Asynchronous message broker facilitating communication between physical twin sensors/actuators and digital twin cyber components (e.g., model solvers, monitors)."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataPreprocessingEnabler, ModelExecutionEnabler
]

instance ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Calibration algorithms adjusting model parameters (e.g., thermal inertia) to minimize discrepancies between simulated and real-world sensor values, ensuring predictive accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelValidationService, PredictiveSimulationService
]

instance DataPreprocessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Preprocessing pipeline applying calibration algorithms and data quality checks to refine raw sensor data before model input."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService
]

instance ModelExecutionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Execution environment for running calibrated thermal models (2-parameter and 4-parameter) to generate simulation outputs under current or hypothetical conditions."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveSimulationService, FaultDetectionService
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous acquisition and aggregation of sensor data (internal/external temperatures) to assess system state and detect deviations from nominal operation."
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureDeviationInsight, SystemStateEstimationInsight
]

instance ModelValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Validation of model accuracy by comparing simulation outputs with real-time sensor data to ensure predictive fidelity."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelAccuracyInsight
]

instance PredictiveSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Execution of dynamic thermal models to reproduce and predict physical twin behavior under current or hypothetical operating conditions."
    DTDFVocab:provides ThermalGradientPredictionInsight, FutureStateForecastInsight
]

instance FaultDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Detection of system anomalies (e.g., sensor drift, heating inefficiencies) through comparison of model predictions with real-time sensor data."
    DTDFVocab:provides FaultDetectionInsight, PerformanceDegradationInsight
]

instance ControlAdjustmentService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Optimization of control actions (e.g., adjusting fan/heatbed duty cycles) based on real-time monitoring and predictive insights to maintain temperature setpoints."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlOptimizationAction
]

instance TemperatureDeviationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification of temperature deviations from setpoints (e.g., internal air temperature exceeding safe thresholds)."
]

instance SystemStateEstimationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Estimation of current system state including thermal gradients and heat distribution within the incubator."
]

instance ModelAccuracyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantitative assessment of model calibration quality through comparison of simulated and real-world temperature trajectories."
]

instance ThermalGradientPredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Prediction of thermal gradients within the incubator using differential equation-based models calibrated to real-time data."
]

instance FutureStateForecastInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Forecast of future temperature trajectories under current operating conditions or hypothetical scenarios (e.g., external temperature fluctuations)."
]

instance FaultDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detection of system faults such as sensor drift, heating inefficiencies, or actuator failures through model-sensor data comparison."
]

instance PerformanceDegradationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification of performance degradation patterns (e.g., increased energy consumption for same temperature regulation)."
]

instance ControlOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated adjustment of fan/heatbed duty cycles to optimize energy efficiency while maintaining temperature setpoints."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance HeatbedActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "PCB-based resistive heating element controlled via digital signals from Raspberry Pi to regulate internal temperature. Operates in tandem with fan for uniform heat distribution."
]

instance FanActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Mechanical fan controlled via Raspberry Pi to ensure uniform temperature distribution within the insulated box by circulating air."
]

instance StyrofoamInsulatedBox : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Insulated container minimizing heat loss and providing thermal mass for stable temperature regulation."
]

instance IncubatorControlSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Cyber-physical system integrating sensors, actuators, and Raspberry Pi controller to achieve closed-loop temperature regulation."
    base:contains HeatbedActuator, FanActuator, InternalAirTemperatureSensor, HeatbedTemperatureSensor, ExternalTemperatureSensor
]

instance ThermalEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Operational environment including ambient temperature fluctuations and internal thermal loads from contents (e.g., biological samples)."
    base:contains ExternalTemperatureSensor
]

instance IncubatorSystem : DTDFVocab:SystemUnderStudy [
    base:desc "Cyber-Physical System for temperature regulation in incubator applications."
    DTDFVocab:hasEnvironment ThermalEnvironment
    base:contains IncubatorControlSystem
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) of the incubator system operates in a **passive monitoring role**, listening to message exchanges between the **Raspberry Pi-based controller** and the **heatbed/driver components** via **RabbitMQ**. The DT does not directly issue control commands to the physical system; instead, it **analyzes the controller's actions** (e.g., bang-bang control logic) and provides **predictive insights** or **diagnostic alerts** (e.g., fault detection, performance violations) to human operators or supervisory systems. The controller, implemented as a **bang-bang controller**, autonomously adjusts the heatbed based on internal temperature sensor feedback, without explicit DT intervention. No validation/authentication mechanisms for control commands are described, as the DT lacks direct actuation authority. Feedback loops exist implicitly via sensor data, but conflict resolution or command confirmation protocols are not specified in the documents."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system for the incubator operates on a periodic synchronization mechanism where temperature data is collected from the Physical Twin (PT) via sensors (two internal and one external) and transmitted to the Digital Twin for model calibration and monitoring. The system does not specify explicit synchronization frequencies or latency requirements, but the controller (Raspberry Pi) periodically reads temperature data to adjust the heatbed and fan operations. The calibration algorithms update the simulation models (with two or four free parameters) using this data, implying a time scale dependent on the sensor polling rate and the thermal inertia of the system. The delayed effect of temperature changes (e.g., thermal inertia of the heatbed) suggests that the system operates on a time scale where the controller must account for transient dynamics, but no sub-second or millisecond-scale updates are explicitly mentioned. The temporal granularity is constrained by the physical system's response time rather than computational constraints."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin constellation for the incubator follows a **CPS-based architecture** (Cyber-Physical System), where the DT integrates three core components in a **feedback-driven orchestration**:
   1. **Data Layer**: Real-time sensor data (temperature) from the PT is streamed to the DT via the Raspberry Pi controller.
   2. **Model Layer**: Two **physics-based thermal models** (differential equation representations) are employed, with **calibration algorithms** dynamically tuning free parameters (2 or 4) to minimize prediction error. The models abstract both the **physical twin (heatbed, fan, insulated box)** and **environmental interactions** (e.g., external temperature).
   3. **Algorithm Layer**: Includes **supervisory controllers** (e.g., PID-like logic for temperature regulation), **fault detectors** (to identify deviations from optimal performance), and **state estimators** (to infer internal states like thermal inertia). The orchestration follows a **centralized control loop**: sensor data → model calibration → monitor evaluation → human/algorithm intervention (if violations are detected). The architecture leverages **open-source hardware/software** (Raspberry Pi, Python-based models) and is designed for **reconfigurability** to adapt to changing environmental conditions (e.g., varying thermal loads)."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle for the described incubator case study includes the following phases: **(1) System Design and Modeling**: The DT is built by integrating models of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and its environment, including both physical (e.g., styrofoam box, heatbed, fan, temperature sensors) and cyber components (e.g., Raspberry Pi controller). Models are developed at varying levels of abstraction, with calibration algorithms used to refine parameters (e.g., thermal dynamics) for accuracy. **(2) Deployment and Integration**: The DT is deployed alongside the Physical Twin (PT), where real-time sensor data (e.g., internal/external temperature measurements) is continuously collected and fed into the DT framework. **(3) Runtime Operation**: The DT operates in parallel with the PT, employing calibration algorithms to update models dynamically, ensuring alignment with the PT’s current state. **(4) Monitoring and Adaptation**: Monitors within the DT assess system performance (e.g., safety, optimality) using the updated models and sensor data. Violations trigger human or algorithmic interventions to adjust the PT (e.g., modifying heating/fan control). **(5) Evolution and Maintenance**: The DT supports reconfigurability to adapt to environmental changes (e.g., varying thermal loads) and enables reduced maintenance through predictive analytics. Governance is implicit via the iterative calibration and monitoring loop, ensuring the DT remains synchronized with the PT’s operational reality. Transition between phases is managed via data-driven model updates and algorithmic decision-making, with no explicit governance framework (e.g., ISO standards) mentioned in the documents."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Model **fidelity** in the Digital Twin is determined by the **number of free parameters** and their alignment with PT behavior. The incubator case study demonstrates that a **4-parameter thermal model** outperformed a **2-parameter model** in predicting **thermal inertia**, validating higher-fidelity requirements for critical applications. **Validation and verification (V&V)** methods include **experimental comparison** between DT simulations and PT measurements (e.g., temperature readings) to quantify discrepancies. **Uncertainty sources** arise from sensor noise, environmental variability (e.g., ambient temperature fluctuations), and model simplifications (e.g., ignoring heatbed material non-linearities). **Error detection** is handled via **calibration algorithms** that iteratively refine model parameters using real-time sensor data, while **quality assurance** relies on **monitoring algorithms** (e.g., fault detectors) to flag deviations from optimal PT performance. The DT framework ensures **traceability** by retaining models post-deployment, enabling continuous V&V throughout the system’s lifecycle. No explicit error correction mechanisms (e.g., feedback control loops) are detailed, but the incubator’s controller suggests **closed-loop adjustments** to mitigate discrepancies."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT's technical network infrastructure relies on a **1-Wire bus** for sensor-to-Raspberry Pi communication, ensuring low-latency data acquisition from the **three temperature sensors**. The Raspberry Pi hosts a **RabbitMQ server** for message queuing between the low-level driver, calibration algorithms, and the DT's simulation models. No explicit details on **bandwidth requirements**, **network topology beyond the bus**, or **security protocols** are provided in the documents. The system assumes **reliability through redundancy** in sensor measurements (two internal sensors) and **periodic calibration** to maintain model accuracy. Latency is not quantified, but the use of a **real-time control loop** suggests sub-second response times for critical feedback."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT's deployment infrastructure is **edge-centric**, with the **Raspberry Pi** serving as the primary host for the **Temperature Controller**, **RabbitMQ server**, and **calibration algorithms**. The system leverages **open-source hardware/software** (available via [GitHub repository](https://github.com/INTO-CPS-Association/example-incubator)), enabling on-premise deployment without cloud dependencies. Computational requirements are minimal, focusing on **real-time sensor polling**, **model calibration**, and **control logic execution**. Scalability mechanisms are not explicitly detailed, though the modular design (separate models for cyber/physical components) suggests potential for distributed extensions. Availability is assured by the **closed-loop feedback system**, which dynamically adjusts to sensor failures (e.g., via redundant internal measurements). No cloud or hybrid deployment is mentioned, but the RabbitMQ-based communication could theoretically support distributed architectures."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system for the incubator case study incorporates **safety-critical considerations** through its integration of **monitoring algorithms** that check for safe and optimal performance of the Physical Twin (PT). The system employs **calibration algorithms** to update models of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and environment, ensuring accurate parameter estimation for reliable operation. **Fail-safe mechanisms** are implied through the DT's ability to detect violations (e.g., temperature deviations or system malfunctions) via monitors, triggering human or algorithmic interventions to adjust the PT. While the documents do not explicitly mention **cybersecurity protocols** (e.g., encryption, access control, or authentication systems), the **Raspberry Pi-based controller** and **sensor data collection** imply a need for secure communication channels (e.g., encrypted sensor-to-controller data transmission) and **access control** to prevent unauthorized modifications to the DT or PT. **Risk assessment** is indirectly addressed through the DT's predictive and simulation capabilities, which allow for preemptive identification of potential failures or unsafe conditions. However, specific compliance with data protection regulations (e.g., GDPR), consent management, or data anonymization is **not in Document**."
]
